headline: OpenAI Funding Round Grows from $120B to $122B, Closes at $852B Valuation
raw_snippet: OpenAI's record funding round grew from $120 billion (announced March 24) to $122 billion when it officially closed on March 31 at an $852 billion post-money valuation. The round was anchored by Amazon ($50B, $35B contingent on IPO/AGI), Nvidia ($30B), and SoftBank ($30B). It also included $3 billion from retail investors via JP Morgan, Morgan Stanley, and Goldman Sachs — the largest retail private placement those banks have handled.
curator_notes: Merged from week 1 (OpenAI Secures Additional $10 Billion, B) and week 2 (OpenAI Closes Record $122 Billion Fundin)
verification_notes: URL returned 403 — likely bot protection, manual verification recommended

headline: OpenAI Foundation Names Leaders, Pledges $1 Billion for AI Safety and Life Sciences
raw_snippet: OpenAI's nonprofit arm named new leaders including co-founder Wojciech Zaremba to lead AI safety work, Jacob Trefethen for life sciences, Robert Kaiden as CFO, and Anna Adeola as Head of AI for Civil Society. The Foundation pledged to spend at least $1 billion in 2026 on protecting society from AI risks like biological threats and funding AI applications in life sciences.
curator_notes: 1B investment pledge and CFO hire.

headline: SoftBank's $40 Billion Unsecured Loan Signals Potential 2026 OpenAI IPO
raw_snippet: SoftBank took on a new unsecured $40 billion loan with a 12-month term to help cover its $30 billion commitment to OpenAI's $110 billion raise. The loan's short maturity is being interpreted as a signal that lenders expect OpenAI's highly anticipated public listing to occur later in 2026, which would allow SoftBank to refinance against its appreciated stake.
curator_notes: Major IPO signal and $40B capital movement.
verification_notes: URL returned 200. Content matches headline keywords.

headline: OpenAI Discloses $2 Billion Monthly Revenue Run Rate and 900 Million Weekly Active Users
raw_snippet: Alongside the record funding round, OpenAI disclosed it is generating $2 billion in monthly revenue with more than 900 million ChatGPT weekly active users and 50+ million paid subscribers. Enterprise now represents >40% of OpenAI's revenue and is on track to reach parity with consumer by year-end. CFO Sarah Friar said the company is growing revenue 4x faster than Alphabet and Meta at comparable stages.
curator_notes: Critical operating metrics for IB audience.

headline: OpenAI Secondary Market Demand Sinks to $765B Valuation Even as Primary Round Hits $852B
raw_snippet: OpenAI shares are trading on secondary markets at an implied ~$765 billion valuation — roughly a 10% discount to the $852 billion price set in the March 31 primary round — as institutional investors pivot to Anthropic. Morgan Stanley and Goldman Sachs have begun offering OpenAI shares to wealth clients without carry fees, while secondary marketplace Next Round Capital said it could not find buyers for $600 million of OpenAI shares from ~six institutions looking to sell. Anthropic is seeing record demand on Augment and Hiive.
curator_notes: Important price-discovery signal — secondary trades at 10% discount to primary round.

headline: CFO Sarah Friar Warns 2026 IPO Timeline Too Aggressive, Reportedly at Odds with Altman
raw_snippet: OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar has reportedly raised internal concerns that the company's Q4 2026 IPO timeline is too aggressive given its $600 billion in cloud capacity commitments and slowing revenue growth. Friar is reportedly at odds with CEO Sam Altman over the timing and was subsequently shifted to report to Fidji Simo instead of Altman directly.
curator_notes: Internal executive tension on IPO timing. Single-source but newsworthy.

headline: OpenAI Pivots ChatGPT Commerce from Instant Checkout to Visual Product Discovery via ACP
raw_snippet: OpenAI abandoned its Instant Checkout feature after poor merchant onboarding and low conversion, pivoting to an Agentic Commerce Protocol (ACP) focused on visual product discovery. By April 2, Target, Sephora, Nordstrom, Best Buy, The Home Depot, and Wayfair had integrated. Users can browse, compare side-by-side, and upload images to find similar items across all ChatGPT tiers.
curator_notes: Merged from week 1 (OpenAI Pivots ChatGPT Shopping from Inst) and week 2 (OpenAI Launches Visual Product Discovery)

headline: OpenAI Shuts Down Sora Video App and API, Citing Unsustainable Economics
raw_snippet: OpenAI announced it is shutting down its Sora video app on April 26, 2026 and the API on September 24, 2026. The company cited high compute costs and poor adoption — the app peaked at 3.3M downloads in November but fell to 1.1M by February, earning just $2.1M lifetime in-app revenue against estimated inference costs of $15M per day. The Sora research team will refocus on world simulation for robotics.
curator_notes: Massive product shutdown and $15M daily inference capex flag.
verification_notes: URL returned 200. Content matches headline keywords.

headline: OpenAI Shelves Adult Mode 'Citron' Chatbot Indefinitely After Employee and Investor Pushback
raw_snippet: OpenAI indefinitely abandoned plans for an adult-oriented chatbot reportedly called 'Citron mode' following concerns from employees, investors, and advisers. The company struggled to train models to allow erotic content while blocking illegal material, and faced worries about exposing minors to explicit content. A senior employee reportedly left over the issue. It is the second product OpenAI cut in a week, following Sora.
curator_notes: Brand capability pivot.

headline: ChatGPT Adds Hands-Free CarPlay Voice Chat for Supported iOS Vehicles
raw_snippet: OpenAI added hands-free CarPlay voice chat support, allowing users on iOS 26.4+ to start and resume ChatGPT voice conversations directly from the CarPlay interface in supported vehicles. The rollout positions ChatGPT as a direct voice competitor to in-car assistants from Apple, Google, and Amazon.

headline: OpenAI Launches Pay-As-You-Go Codex Seats for ChatGPT Business and Enterprise
raw_snippet: OpenAI introduced Codex-only seats on pay-as-you-go pricing for ChatGPT Business and Enterprise. The new tier has no rate limits and is billed per-token via a new credit-based rate card matching API pricing. Usage on Codex within ChatGPT Business has grown 6x since January, with more than 2 million weekly Codex users overall.
curator_notes: Tightly clustered with the $20/seat price cut.

headline: OpenAI Announces Retirement of Standard Voice Mode Within 30 Days
raw_snippet: OpenAI said it will retire Standard Voice Mode in 30 days, unifying all ChatGPT users onto Advanced Voice Mode. Plus users get near-unlimited access; Free users get several hours per day.
curator_notes: Notable product sunset with workflow impact for paid users.

headline: Disney Exits Planned $1 Billion OpenAI Investment After Sora Shutdown
raw_snippet: Disney dropped its planned $1 billion investment in OpenAI and the licensing deal that allowed its characters in Sora-generated videos, following OpenAI's decision to shut down Sora. No money had actually changed hands as the deal was never finalized. A Disney spokesperson said they 'respect OpenAI's decision to exit the video generation business.'
curator_notes: Major $1B blue-chip deal collapse.

headline: OpenAI Acquires TBPN, First Media Company Acquisition, to Expand Tech-Industry Voice
raw_snippet: OpenAI announced the acquisition of Technology Business Programming Network (TBPN), the daily live tech talk show hosted by Jordi Hays and John Coogan, marking OpenAI's first acquisition of a media company. TBPN will maintain editorial independence but report to OpenAI's chief political operative Chris Lehane. The show is on track to pull in more than $30 million in annual revenue per the Wall Street Journal.
curator_notes: First media acquisition; clustered with IPO-context angle.

headline: OpenAI Releases Open-Source Teen Safety Policy Tools for Developers
raw_snippet: OpenAI released open-source teen safety policies for use with its gpt-oss-safeguard model, developed in collaboration with Common Sense Media and everyone.ai. The policies address graphic violence, harmful body ideals, dangerous challenges, romantic roleplay, and age-restricted content, released through the ROOST Model Community to encourage industry-wide adoption.

headline: iyO Amends Lawsuit Against OpenAI's io Hardware Venture, Alleges Trade Secret Theft
raw_snippet: Audio startup iyO filed an amended complaint against OpenAI's hardware venture io, escalating from trademark infringement to trade secret theft. iyO alleges a former engineer downloaded confidential CAD files and proprietary designs and shared them with io co-founder Tang Tan (former Apple designer), claiming this accelerated io's product development by nearly a decade.

headline: OpenAI Hires Former Meta VP Dave Dugan to Lead ChatGPT Advertising Business
raw_snippet: OpenAI hired Dave Dugan, former VP of global clients and agencies at Meta, as VP of global ad solutions reporting to COO Brad Lightcap. His priority is transitioning ChatGPT ads from pilot to commercial-ready. OpenAI is asking a minimum $200,000 commitment from ad partners, with early brands including AppLovin, WIRED, and Best Buy already running pilot programs. The hire marks a reversal from Altman's prior stance that advertising was a 'last resort.'
curator_notes: Signals strong ad business push.

headline: OpenAI Hires JioStar CEO Kiran Mani as Asia-Pacific Managing Director
raw_snippet: OpenAI hired Kiran Mani, CEO of Indian streaming platform JioStar, for a newly created role as managing director of Asia-Pacific operations starting in June. Mani, who spent 13+ years at Google leading Android and Google Play across APAC, will relocate to OpenAI's Singapore office and report to Chief Strategy Officer Jason Kwon. India is OpenAI's second-largest market with over 100M weekly ChatGPT users.
curator_notes: Major regional head hire for top secondary market.

headline: OpenAI Product & Business Chief Fidji Simo Takes Multi-Week Medical Leave
raw_snippet: Fidji Simo, OpenAI's CEO of Applications, told staff on Friday she is taking several weeks of medical leave to manage a worsening neuroimmune condition (POTS). President Greg Brockman will oversee product — including the new desktop super app project — in her absence.
curator_notes: Cluster of 4 distinct executive changes on the same day. Primary item describes Simo's leave; the rest are clustered.

headline: OpenAI in Advanced Talks to Buy Fusion Power from Helion Energy; Altman Steps Down from Helion Board
raw_snippet: OpenAI is in advanced talks to buy electricity from Sam Altman-backed fusion startup Helion Energy, targeting 5 gigawatts by 2030 scaling to 50 gigawatts by 2035, securing a guaranteed 12.5% of Helion's production. Altman stepped down as Helion's board chair and recused himself from deal discussions to avoid conflicts of interest. Site selection has not yet been completed.
curator_notes: Massive energy capex deal (50GW).
